auth-authform.name: AuthForm
auth-authform.import:
import { AuthForm, StorybookAuthProvider } from "vendor";
import { View } from "react-native";

param: props - Determines which auth flow content and behavior to render.
returns: The composed auth screen for login or registration.

auth-authform.description: Renders the shared auth screen used by the login and registration routes.

type.description: Selects whether the shared form renders the login or register flow.

auth-authheader.name: AuthHeader

stories.name: Login
stories.snippet: const Login = () => <AuthHeader heading="Welcome back!" emailPrompt="or sign in with your email" />;

stories.name: Register
stories.snippet: const Register = () => <AuthHeader heading="Create your Account" emailPrompt="or sign up with your email" />;

auth-authheader.import:
import { AuthHeader } from "vendor";
import { View } from "react-native";

param: props - Heading and supporting copy for the auth header.
returns: The auth header with a social sign-in placeholder and copy.

auth-authheader.description: Renders the shared header used by the login and registration screens.

heading.description: Primary heading rendered at the top of the auth screen.

emailPrompt.description: Supporting copy shown above the email form fields.

auth-authheading.name: AuthHeading

stories.name: Centered
stories.snippet: const Centered = () => <AuthHeading label="Recover your password" className="text-center" />;

auth-authheading.import:
import { AuthHeading } from "vendor";
import { View } from "react-native";

param: props - Label text plus passthrough text props.

auth-authheading.description: Renders the shared large-format heading used across auth screens.

label.description: Heading text rendered by the component.

auth-authlayout.name: AuthLayout

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet:
const Default = () => (
  <View style={{ width: 420, height: 760 }}>
    <AuthLayout>
      <View style={{ gap: 20 }}>
        <AuthHeading className="text-center" label="Layout container" />
        <Text className="text-center leading-6 text-muted-foreground">
          The auth layout centers a single form card inside the shared safe
          screen wrapper.
        </Text>
      </View>
    </AuthLayout>
  </View>
);

auth-authlayout.import:
import { AuthHeading, AuthLayout, Text } from "vendor";
import { View } from "react-native";

auth-resendcodebutton.name: ResendCodeButton

stories.name: Countdown
stories.snippet: const Countdown = () => <ResendCodeButton seconds={42} isRunning handleResendCode={async () => undefined} />;

stories.name: Ready To Resend
stories.snippet: const ReadyToResend = () => <ResendCodeButton seconds={0} isRunning={false} handleResendCode={async () => undefined} />;

param: props - Timer state and resend callback for the OTP flow.
returns: Cooldown feedback while running, otherwise a resend button.

auth-resendcodebutton.description: Renders either the resend cooldown text or the resend action button.

seconds.description: Remaining seconds in the resend cooldown.

isRunning.description: Indicates whether the cooldown timer is still active.

handleResendCode.description: Callback fired when the user requests a new OTP code.

shared-errortext.name: ErrorText

stories.name: Visible
stories.snippet: const Visible = () => <ErrorText isShown label="Something went wrong. Please try again." />;

stories.name: Hidden
stories.snippet: const Hidden = () => <ErrorText isShown={false} label="This should not render" />;

shared-errortext.import:
import { ErrorText } from "vendor";
import { View } from "react-native";

param: props - Visibility flag, message content, and passthrough text props.
returns: The styled error message when visible; otherwise nothing.

label.description: Error message to render when `isShown` is true.

isShown.description: Controls whether the error message should be rendered.

shared-safeview.name: SafeView

stories.name: Default
stories.snippet:
const Default = () => (
  <View style={{ width: 390, height: 720 }}>
    <SafeView>
      <View
        style={{
          flex: 1,
          borderRadius: 24,
          borderWidth: 1,
          borderColor: "rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.08)",
          backgroundColor: "rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.88)",
          padding: 24,
          justifyContent: "space-between",
        }}
      >
        <View style={{ gap: 8 }}>
          <Text className="text-xs uppercase tracking-widest text-primary">
            Screen shell
          </Text>
          <Text size="2xl" bold className="text-foreground">
            Shared safe-area wrapper
          </Text>
          <Text className="leading-6 text-muted-foreground">
            SafeView owns the ambient gradient and the screen padding used by
            the current auth flow.
          </Text>
        </View>
        <View
          style={{
            borderRadius: 20,
            backgroundColor: "#eff6ff",
            padding: 16,
          }}
        >
          <Text className="text-sm text-foreground">
            Content is rendered inside the device-safe viewport.
          </Text>
        </View>
      </View>
    </SafeView>
  </View>
);

shared-safeview.import:
import { SafeView, Text } from "vendor";
import { View } from "react-native";

param: props - Child content and optional layout classes.
returns: The safe-area screen wrapper used by app pages.

shared-safeview.description: Wraps a screen in the shared safe-area container and top gradient treatment.

children.description: Child subtree rendered inside the safe area wrapper.

className.description: Optional utility classes applied to the inner safe-area container.

shared-submitbutton.name: SubmitButton

stories.name: Idle
stories.snippet: const Idle = () => <SubmitButton label="Sign in" loading={false} onPress={fn()} />;

stories.name: Loading
stories.snippet: const Loading = () => <SubmitButton label="Sign in" loading onPress={fn()} />;

stories.name: Disabled
stories.snippet: const Disabled = () => <SubmitButton label="Continue" loading={false} onPress={fn()} isDisabled />;

shared-submitbutton.import:
import { SubmitButton } from "vendor";
import { View } from "react-native";

param: props - Button props plus loading state and label text.
returns: A styled button with optional loading feedback.

shared-submitbutton.description: Renders the shared submit button used by auth forms.

loading.description: Indicates whether the button should display a loading spinner and loading label.

label.description: Visible label rendered inside the button when not loading.
